# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - build
  - test
  - staging
  - production

variables:
  APP_NAME: "myapp"
  BUILD_DIR: "build_output"

# Build stage - compile binaries and prepare configs
build_binaries:
  stage: build
  image: golang:1.21
  script:
    - echo "Building application binaries..."
    - mkdir -p ${BUILD_DIR}/bin
    - mkdir -p ${BUILD_DIR}/configs
    - mkdir -p ${BUILD_DIR}/migrations
    - go build -o ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME} ./cmd/main.go
    - go build -o ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli ./cmd/cli/main.go
    - cp -r configs/* ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/
    - cp -r db/migrations/* ${BUILD_DIR}/migrations/
    - echo "BUILD_VERSION=$(git describe --tags --always)" > ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - echo "BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - echo "BUILD_COMMIT=${CI_COMMIT_SHA}" >> ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - chmod +x ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/*
  artifacts:
    name: "${APP_NAME}-binaries-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/migrations/
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    expire_in: 30 days
    reports:
      dotenv: ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env

build_docker_image:
  stage: build
  image: docker:24.0
  services:
    - docker:24.0-dind
  variables:
    DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR: "/certs"
  script:
    - echo "Building Docker image..."
    - docker build -t ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} .
    - docker tag ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest
    - echo "${CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}" | docker login -u "${CI_REGISTRY_USER}" --password-stdin ${CI_REGISTRY}
    - docker push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}
    - docker push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest
    - echo "DOCKER_IMAGE=${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}" > docker_image.env
  artifacts:
    name: "docker-image-info-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - docker_image.env
    expire_in: 30 days
    reports:
      dotenv: docker_image.env

generate_manifests:
  stage: build
  image: alpine:3.18
  script:
    - echo "Generating deployment manifests..."
    - mkdir -p manifests/staging manifests/production
    - |
      cat > manifests/staging/deployment.yaml <<EOF
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
        name: ${APP_NAME}
        namespace: staging
      spec:
        replicas: 2
        selector:
          matchLabels:
            app: ${APP_NAME}
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ${APP_NAME}
          spec:
            containers:
              - name: ${APP_NAME}
                image: DOCKER_IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER
                ports:
                  - containerPort: 8080
                envFrom:
                  - configMapRef:
                      name: ${APP_NAME}-config
      EOF
    - |
      cat > manifests/production/deployment.yaml <<EOF
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
        name: ${APP_NAME}
        namespace: production
      spec:
        replicas: 4
        selector:
          matchLabels:
            app: ${APP_NAME}
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ${APP_NAME}
          spec:
            containers:
              - name: ${APP_NAME}
                image: DOCKER_IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER
                ports:
                  - containerPort: 8080
                envFrom:
                  - configMapRef:
                      name: ${APP_NAME}-config
      EOF
    - |
      cat > manifests/staging/configmap.yaml <<EOF
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: ConfigMap
      metadata:
        name: ${APP_NAME}-config
        namespace: staging
      data:
        APP_ENV: staging
        LOG_LEVEL: debug
      EOF
    - |
      cat > manifests/production/configmap.yaml <<EOF
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: ConfigMap
      metadata:
        name: ${APP_NAME}-config
        namespace: production
      data:
        APP_ENV: production
        LOG_LEVEL: info
      EOF
  artifacts:
    name: "manifests-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - manifests/
    expire_in: 30 days

# Test stage - downloads build artifacts and runs tests
unit_tests:
  stage: test
  image: golang:1.21
  needs:
    - job: build_binaries
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Running unit tests using build artifacts..."
    - source ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - echo "Testing build version ${BUILD_VERSION} from ${BUILD_DATE}"
    - ls -la ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/
    - go test -v -race -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
    - go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o coverage.html
  artifacts:
    name: "test-results-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - coverage.out
      - coverage.html
    expire_in: 30 days
    reports:
      coverage_report:
        coverage_format: cobertura
        path: coverage.out

integration_tests:
  stage: test
  image: golang:1.21
  services:
    - postgres:15
    - redis:7
  variables:
    POSTGRES_DB: testdb
    POSTGRES_USER: testuser
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD: testpass
    DATABASE_URL: "postgres://testuser:testpass@postgres:5432/testdb?sslmode=disable"
    REDIS_URL: "redis://redis:6379"
  needs:
    - job: build_binaries
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Running integration tests with build artifacts..."
    - source ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - echo "Testing commit ${BUILD_COMMIT}"
    - ls -la ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/
    - ls -la ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/
    - |
      echo "Running database migrations..."
      ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli migrate --config ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/test.yaml --migrations-dir ${BUILD_DIR}/migrations/
    - |
      echo "Starting application in background for integration tests..."
      ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME} --config ${BUILD_DIR}/configs/test.yaml &
      APP_PID=$!
      sleep 5
    - |
      echo "Running integration test suite..."
      go test -v -tags=integration ./tests/integration/...
      TEST_EXIT_CODE=$?
    - kill $APP_PID || true
    - exit $TEST_EXIT_CODE
  artifacts:
    name: "integration-test-results-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - tests/integration/reports/
    expire_in: 30 days
    when: always

smoke_test_binary:
  stage: test
  image: alpine:3.18
  needs:
    - job: build_binaries
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Smoke testing compiled binaries..."
    - source ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - echo "Verifying build ${BUILD_VERSION}"
    - test -x ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}
    - test -x ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli
    - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME} --version
    - ${BUILD_DIR}/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli --help
    - echo "All binaries are valid and executable"

# Staging deployment - downloads artifacts from build and test stages
deploy_staging:
  stage: staging
  image: bitnami/kubectl:1.28
  environment:
    name: staging
    url: https://staging.example.com
  needs:
    - job: build_binaries
      artifacts: true
    - job: build_docker_image
      artifacts: true
    - job: generate_manifests
      artifacts: true
    - job: unit_tests
      artifacts: true
    - job: integration_tests
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Deploying to staging environment..."
    - source ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - echo "Deploying version ${BUILD_VERSION} (commit ${BUILD_COMMIT}) built at ${BUILD_DATE}"
    - echo "Using Docker image ${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
    - |
      echo "Updating image reference in manifests..."
      sed -i "s|DOCKER_IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER|${DOCKER_IMAGE}|g" manifests/staging/deployment.yaml
    - |
      echo "Applying Kubernetes manifests..."
      kubectl apply -f manifests/staging/configmap.yaml
      kubectl apply -f manifests/staging/deployment.yaml
    - |
      echo "Running database migrations on staging..."
      kubectl exec -n staging deployment/${APP_NAME} -- \
        /app/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli migrate --config /app/configs/staging.yaml
    - |
      echo "Waiting for rollout to complete..."
      kubectl rollout status deployment/${APP_NAME} -n staging --timeout=300s
    - |
      echo "Verifying deployment health..."
      kubectl get pods -n staging -l app=${APP_NAME}
    - echo "STAGING_DEPLOYED=true" > staging_status.env
    - echo "STAGING_DEPLOY_TIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> staging_status.env
  artifacts:
    name: "staging-deployment-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - staging_status.env
      - manifests/staging/
    expire_in: 30 days
    reports:
      dotenv: staging_status.env
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main" || $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"

staging_smoke_test:
  stage: staging
  image: curlimages/curl:8.4.0
  needs:
    - job: deploy_staging
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Running smoke tests against staging..."
    - source staging_status.env
    - echo "Staging was deployed at ${STAGING_DEPLOY_TIME}"
    - |
      echo "Checking health endpoint..."
      HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://staging.example.com/health)
      if [ "$HTTP_STATUS" != "200" ]; then
        echo "Health check failed with status $HTTP_STATUS"
        exit 1
      fi
    - |
      echo "Checking readiness endpoint..."
      curl -sf https://staging.example.com/ready
    - echo "Staging smoke tests passed"
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main" || $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "develop"

# Production deployment - downloads artifacts and deploys with manual approval
deploy_production:
  stage: production
  image: bitnami/kubectl:1.28
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://app.example.com
  needs:
    - job: build_binaries
      artifacts: true
    - job: build_docker_image
      artifacts: true
    - job: generate_manifests
      artifacts: true
    - job: deploy_staging
      artifacts: true
    - job: staging_smoke_test
  script:
    - echo "Deploying to production environment..."
    - source ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - echo "Production deployment of version ${BUILD_VERSION} (commit ${BUILD_COMMIT})"
    - echo "This build was staged at ${STAGING_DEPLOY_TIME}"
    - echo "Using Docker image ${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
    - |
      echo "Creating pre-deployment backup..."
      kubectl get deployment ${APP_NAME} -n production -o yaml > manifests/production/previous_deployment_backup.yaml || true
    - |
      echo "Updating image reference in production manifests..."
      sed -i "s|DOCKER_IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER|${DOCKER_IMAGE}|g" manifests/production/deployment.yaml
    - |
      echo "Applying production Kubernetes manifests..."
      kubectl apply -f manifests/production/configmap.yaml
      kubectl apply -f manifests/production/deployment.yaml
    - |
      echo "Running database migrations on production..."
      kubectl exec -n production deployment/${APP_NAME} -- \
        /app/bin/${APP_NAME}-cli migrate --config /app/configs/production.yaml
    - |
      echo "Waiting for production rollout to complete..."
      kubectl rollout status deployment/${APP_NAME} -n production --timeout=600s
    - |
      echo "Verifying production deployment..."
      kubectl get pods -n production -l app=${APP_NAME}
      READY_PODS=$(kubectl get pods -n production -l app=${APP_NAME} --field-selector=status.phase=Running --no-headers | wc -l)
      if [ "$READY_PODS" -lt 4 ]; then
        echo "Expected at least 4 running pods, found $READY_PODS"
        exit 1
      fi
    - echo "PRODUCTION_DEPLOYED=true" > production_status.env
    - echo "PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_TIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> production_status.env
    - echo "PRODUCTION_VERSION=${BUILD_VERSION}" >> production_status.env
  artifacts:
    name: "production-deployment-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    paths:
      - production_status.env
      - manifests/production/
      - ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    expire_in: 30 days
    reports:
      dotenv: production_status.env
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
      when: manual
      allow_failure: false

production_verification:
  stage: production
  image: curlimages/curl:8.4.0
  needs:
    - job: deploy_production
      artifacts: true
    - job: build_binaries
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Running production verification..."
    - source production_status.env
    - source ${BUILD_DIR}/build_info.env
    - echo "Verifying production deployment of version ${PRODUCTION_VERSION} deployed at ${PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_TIME}"
    - |
      echo "Health check..."
      HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://app.example.com/health)
      if [ "$HTTP_STATUS" != "200" ]; then
        echo "Production health check FAILED with status $HTTP_STATUS"
        exit 1
      fi
      echo "Health check passed (HTTP $HTTP_STATUS)"
    - |
      echo "Version verification..."
      DEPLOYED_VERSION=$(curl -s https://app.example.com/version | grep -o '"version":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
      echo "Deployed version: $DEPLOYED_VERSION, Expected: ${BUILD_VERSION}"
    - |
      echo "Readiness check..."
      curl -sf https://app.example.com/ready
    - |
      echo "Critical endpoints check..."
      for endpoint in /api/v1/status /api/v1/info; do
        STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://app.example.com${endpoint})
        echo "Endpoint ${endpoint}: HTTP ${STATUS}"
        if [ "$STATUS" -ge 500 ]; then
          echo "CRITICAL: Endpoint ${endpoint} returning server error"
          exit 1
        fi
      done
    - echo "All production verification checks passed successfully"
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"

rollback_production:
  stage: production
  image: bitnami/kubectl:1.28
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://app.example.com
  needs:
    - job: deploy_production
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Rolling back production deployment..."
    - |
      if [ -f manifests/production/previous_deployment_backup.yaml ]; then
        echo "Restoring previous deployment from backup..."
        kubectl apply -f manifests/production/previous_deployment_backup.yaml
      else
        echo "No backup found, using kubectl rollout undo..."
        kubectl rollout undo deployment/${APP_NAME} -n production
      fi
    - kubectl rollout status deployment/${APP_NAME} -n production --timeout=300s
    - echo "Rollback completed successfully"
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
      when: manual
      allow_failure: true
